windows.transcript: Now if you're using the wick that's directional, this method is also quite handy because you can put just a color paper clip on the end to remind yourself which is the
windows.old_description: Overhead: a raw cotton wick is dipped into a jar of melted wax and pulled taut to prime/wax it.
windows.embed_text_v2: waxing raw wick: short glass jar holding clear melted wax on aluminum foil, hand holds cotton wick end marked with green paper clip to track directional wick
windows.summary_v2: A jar of melted wax sits on foil while a hand presents a wick marked with a green paper clip, demonstrating clipping a colored marker on a directional wick.

seconds_v2.desc: A short squat glass jar holding a shallow pool of clear pale-yellow molten wax sits on crinkled aluminum foil against a white tiled wall.
Same jar of clear melted wax resting on the foil, steady shot.
The wax-filled jar sits on the foil sheet, no movement yet.
Close-up holds on the glass jar with its thin layer of liquid wax on the foil.
A fingertip with a hint of colored item appears at the far left edge as the jar of wax stays centered.
A hand enters from the left holding a green-coiled paper clip attached to the end of a wick, near the jar of melted wax.
The hand holds the wick with its green paper-clip marker close to the foil beside the wax jar.
Hand withdraws slightly; the jar of clear wax remains on the foil.
The squat jar of pale liquid wax sits alone again on the aluminum foil against the white tile.
